rulings.Case Title: HOME VEST CAPITAL LLC VS. CHINA ELLIS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Response To Post-Judgment Interrogatories
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, January 30, 2015, Line 1, PLAINTIFF HOME VEST CAPITAL LLC's Motion To Compel Response To Post-Judgment Interrogatories. Pro Tem Judge Robert Kane, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Motion to compel granted 15 days from date order served to file responses. No sanctions as no opposition was filed as required per CCP §2030.290(c). Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to rkane1089@aol.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: 2900 22ND STREET OWNERS' ASSOCIATION VS. SANTANA REAL ESTATE DEVELOPMENT, LLC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Against Plaintiffs Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, January 30, 2015, Line 4, INTERVENOR NORTH AMERICAN SPECIALTY INSURANCE COMPANY MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Against Plaintiffs Complaint. The motion for summary judgment is denied. First, NASIC fails to maintain its burden of production. NASIC relies on paragraph 7 of the declaration of counsel (Mr. Kim) for the proposition that the developer transferred all of its assets to plaintiff. The declaration does not support that assertion and the most that Mr. Kim shows is that the developer "created" plaintiff. Defendant does not show that the developer's assets and liabilities were transferred to plaintiff or that plaintiff is the successor in interest of the developer. NASIC therefore fails to show that plaintiff is subject to the cited contract. Second, the declarations of Mr. Bartak (board member of plaintiff) and Mr. Santana (member of developer) state that the developer never transferred any assets or liabilities to plaintiff. Those declarations create a triable issue of material fact regarding whether plaintiff is subject to the contract. The court denies the sanctions request. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order in compliance with CCP 437c(g) with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: CHERYL SHANKS VS. WALGREEN CO. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT /Adjudication And Index Of Evidence
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, January 30, 2015, Line 6, DEFENDANT WALGREEN CO., JORGE BLANCO, ALEX ORTEGA MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT / Adjudication And Index Of Evidence. Defendants' motion for summary adjudication is granted. As to the Violation of the Unruh Civil Rights Act and Ralph Civil Rights Act causes of action, Defendants met their initial burden of presenting evidence that the employees' conduct was not motivated by race. The evidence submitted by Defendants establishes that the employees only began to watch Plaintiff after another employee witnessed Plaintiff put merchandise in her bag and the employees only attempted to restrain Plaintiff until she began to act aggressively. (Defendant's Undisputed Material Facts ("UMF") 4-6, 13-16.) Plaintiff does not submit evidence raising a triable issue of material fact that Defendants' conduct was motivated by race. Plaintiff's testimony that she spoke with other unidentified African-American friends and acquaintances who told her they felt they were unfairly watched by Walgreens employees is insufficient to raise a triable issue of material fact, as it is speculative and based on hearsay. As to the punitive damages claim, Defendants met their initial burden by presenting evidence showing that Defendants Blanco and Ortega's conduct is protected by the Merchant's Privilege, as set forth in Cal. Penal Code sec. 490.5(f). (UMF 4-6, 13-16.) Plaintiff does not present clear and convincing evidence raising a triable issue of material fact that Defendants Blanco and Ortega did not have probable cause to suspect Plaintiff of shoplifting. Additionally, Plaintiff does not present clear and convincing evidence raising a triable issue of material fact as to whether Defendants Blanco and Ortega's conduct went beyond "a reasonable amount of nondeadly force necessary to protect [themselves] and to prevent escape of the person detained or the loss of tangible or intangible property." (Cal. Penal Code sec. 490.5(f)(2).) The Court sustains Defendants' objection to the surveillance video. The surveillance video was not properly authenticated by an individual with personal knowledge of the incident. (Cal. Evid. Code secs. 702(a), 1400, 1401.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order in compliance with CCP 437c(g) with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: CARLETTA JACKSON-LANE VS. AARON KAPLAN et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To File First Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, January 30, 2015, Line 9, PLAINTIFF CARLETTA JACKSON-LANE Motion For Leave To File First Amended Complaint. Motion granted. Defendant has been aware since July 2014 that Plaintiff intended to amend the Complaint, and Plaintiff was, to a degree, complicit in the delay. (See, e.g., Silverstein Decl. 4 [Defendant sought additional time to consider the stipulation]; see also id. 5 [Defendant requested further information before responding to Plaintiff's request to stipulate].) Further, California adheres to a liberal policy in favor of permitting amendments to the Complaint. Both parties' requests regarding the trial date are denied without prejudice. The parties may seek a trial continuance before the Presiding Judge in Department 206. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: BRYAN LOCKWOOD VS. TAVISTOCK RESTAURANTS, LLC A DELAWARE LIMITED ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, January 30, 2015, Line 11, DEFENDANT TAVISTOCK RESTAURANTS, LLC DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Overruled. The First Amended Complaint ("FAC") adequately pleads the elements of breach of an oral contract. (FAC, pars. 6, 10, 12-13.) This cause of action is not uncertain. Rare One LLC is not an indispensable party. The FAC pleads that Plaintiff and his wife control Rare One LLC. (FAC, par. 2.) As such, Plaintiff can protect Rare One LLC's interests. (See CCP sec. 389; Citizens Assn. for Sensible Development of Bishop Area v. County of Inyo (1985) 172 Cal.App.3d 151, 161.) The FAC also adequately pleads the elements of specific performance. (FAC, pars. 14-21; see Coodwest Rubber Corp. v. Munoz (1985) 170 Cal.App.3d 919, 921.) Defendant's request for judicial notice of the four LLC Operating Agreements and one unexecuted Agreement and Release is denied. The terms and legal effect of a contract are not facts that are not reasonably subject to dispute. (See Evid. Code sec. 452(h).) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: SBERLO FAMILY TRUST VS. TANYA PHILLIPS (3/19/12-AMENDED TO CONTRACT ACTION)

The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. =(302/JPT)
